title: [Phase 1.1] Create Containerfile for Gentoo test environment
body:
Create a `Containerfile` (Podman-style Dockerfile) that:
- Starts from `gentoo/stage3:latest`
- Syncs the portage tree using `emerge-webrsync`
- Installs overlay tooling: `app-portage/repoman`, `dev-util/pkgcheck`, `dev-util/pkgdev`
- Configures the overlay mount point at `/var/db/repos/adaptive-overlay`
- Sets up `repos.conf` for the overlay

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §6.2

title: [Phase 1.2] Set up GHCR publishing workflow for container image
body:
Create a GitHub Actions workflow that:
- Builds the Gentoo test environment container image
- Publishes it to GHCR (GitHub Container Registry)
- Runs weekly to rebuild with updated portage tree
- Supports manual dispatch for on-demand rebuilds

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §6.3

title: [Phase 1.3] Create lint-ebuild reusable workflow
body:
Create `.github/workflows/lint-ebuild.yml` as a reusable workflow that:
- Accepts input parameter: `package_dir` (e.g., `media-sound/carla`)
- Starts the Gentoo container
- Runs `pkgcheck scan <package_dir>`
- Runs `ebuild ./<file>.ebuild manifest` (with fallback to `pkgdev manifest`)
- Reports lint errors and warnings

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §7.1

title: [Phase 1.4] Create scripts/lint.sh for local use
body:
Create `scripts/lint.sh` that:
- Accepts a package directory as argument
- Invokes the Gentoo container (Podman first, Docker fallback)
- Runs pkgcheck inside the container
- Works identically to the CI lint workflow for local development

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §9.1, §9.2

title: [Phase 1.5] Add CI workflow that lints changed ebuilds on every PR
body:
Create a CI workflow that:
- Triggers on pull requests
- Detects changed ebuild files
- Calls the `lint-ebuild` reusable workflow for each changed package
- Reports results as PR checks

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §7.1

title: [Phase 2.1] Create test-ebuild reusable workflow
body:
Create `.github/workflows/test-ebuild.yml` as a reusable workflow that:
- Accepts inputs: `package_dir`, `ebuild_file`
- Starts Gentoo container with cached portage tree and distfiles
- Runs `ebuild ./<ebuild_file> clean compile`
- Verifies build output (binaries exist, version strings, `ldd` linkage)
- Runs final integration test: `emerge --oneshot <category>/<package>` in container
- Uploads build log as artifact

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §5.4, §7.2

title: [Phase 2.2] Create scripts/test-build.sh for local use
body:
Create `scripts/test-build.sh` that:
- Accepts package directory and ebuild file as arguments
- Invokes the Gentoo container (Podman/Docker)
- Runs `ebuild ./<file> clean compile` inside the container
- Matches the CI test-ebuild workflow behavior for local development

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §9.1, §9.2

title: [Phase 2.3] Integrate build testing into PR CI pipeline
body:
Extend the PR CI workflow to:
- Detect changed ebuild files
- Call the `test-ebuild` reusable workflow for each changed ebuild
- Report build test results as PR checks

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §7.2

title: [Phase 2.4] Implement distfiles and portage tree caching
body:
Optimize CI performance by:
- Caching the portage tree between workflow runs
- Caching downloaded distfiles (source tarballs)
- Implementing cache invalidation strategy (weekly refresh)

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §6.3

title: [Phase 3.1] Create .agent/packages.json with upstream metadata
body:
Create `.agent/packages.json` containing:
- Upstream tracking metadata for all current packages
- Fields: category, name, upstream_repo, upstream_type, version_pattern, repackage, auto_upgrade
- Initial entries for: app-editors/zed, media-sound/surgext, and other existing packages

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §5.2, §2.1

title: [Phase 3.2] Create check-upstream-versions workflow
body:
Create a workflow/script that:
- Reads `.agent/packages.json`
- Scans the overlay tree to derive current versions from ebuild filenames
- Queries upstream sources (GitHub Releases API, PyPI, etc.) for latest versions
- Compares and reports packages with available updates

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §5.1, §7.4

title: [Phase 3.3] Create scripts/check-updates.sh for local use
body:
Create `scripts/check-updates.sh` that:
- Implements the version-check logic locally
- Prints packages that have upstream updates
- Works without requiring GitHub Actions environment

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §9.1

title: [Phase 3.4] Set up scheduled cron trigger for version checking
body:
Configure scheduled workflow that:
- Runs twice weekly (or configurable frequency)
- Executes the check-upstream-versions workflow
- Sends notifications on new versions found

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §5.1

title: [Phase 4.1] Create upgrade-ebuild.yml workflow with dispatch inputs
body:
Create `.github/workflows/upgrade-ebuild.yml` that:
- Accepts workflow_dispatch inputs: package, version
- Creates upgrade branch: `upgrade/<category>/<name>-<version>`
- Orchestrates the autonomous upgrade process

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §5.1, §5.3

title: [Phase 4.2] Implement copy-and-update ebuild logic with dependency detection
body:
Implement the ebuild upgrade logic that:
- Copies latest existing ebuild to new version
- Detects dependency changes by diffing upstream manifests (Cargo.toml, CMakeLists.txt, etc.)
- Updates GIT_CRATES for Rust packages
- Updates DEPEND/BDEPEND as needed
- Regenerates Manifest

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §5.3 (step 4-6), §5.5

title: [Phase 4.3] Integrate lint + ebuild build test into upgrade workflow
body:
Enhance the upgrade-ebuild workflow to:
- Run lint-ebuild reusable workflow
- Run test-ebuild reusable workflow
- Implement retry logic on build failures
- Use `ebuild` for iteration, `emerge` only for final container test

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §5.3 (step 7-10), §5.4

title: [Phase 4.4] Implement auto-PR creation with appropriate labels
body:
Enhance the upgrade workflow to:
- Open a PR when lint and build succeed
- Apply appropriate labels (auto-upgrade, needs-review, etc.)
- Implement auto-merge logic for packages with `auto_upgrade: true`
- Send notifications on failure

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §5.3 (step 11-12)

title: [Phase 4.5] Generalize repackage-surge.yml into reusable workflow
body:
Refactor the existing `repackage-surge.yml` into `.github/workflows/repackage-source.yml`:
- Make it accept parameters: upstream_repo, version, tag_pattern, tarball_prefix, etc.
- Clone upstream with submodules
- Create tarball excluding .git directories
- Create GitHub Release with tarball attached
- Support packages with `repackage: true` in packages.json

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §5.6, §7.3

title: [Phase 4.6] Wire version-check to automatically trigger upgrades
body:
Connect the check-upstream-versions workflow to:
- Automatically dispatch the upgrade-ebuild workflow for packages with new versions
- Handle repackaging workflow first when `repackage: true`
- Implement rate limiting and error handling

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §5.3, §7.4

title: [Phase 5.1] Create .agent/ebuild_guidelines.md
body:
Create `.agent/ebuild_guidelines.md` documenting:
- Overlay-specific ebuild conventions
- Preferred USE flags and naming patterns
- Licensing practices
- Manifest handling procedures
- Examples of common ebuild patterns (cmake, cargo, binary repackage)

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §8.3

title: [Phase 5.2] Create scripts/new-ebuild.sh skeleton scaffolder
body:
Create `scripts/new-ebuild.sh` that:
- Accepts category, package name, and version as arguments
- Creates package directory structure
- Generates skeleton ebuild file
- Creates initial metadata.xml
- Provides a starting point for new ebuild development

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §9.1

title: [Phase 5.3] Define new-ebuild.yml workflow with issue/dispatch triggers
body:
Create `.github/workflows/new-ebuild.yml` that:
- Triggers on issue assignment or workflow_dispatch
- Accepts inputs: category, package_name, version, upstream_url
- Orchestrates collaborative ebuild creation
- Implements the agent workflow from planning/workflow_master_plan.md §4.2

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §4.1, §4.2

title: [Phase 5.4] Implement human-in-the-loop mechanism
body:
Implement collaboration mechanisms:
- PR comment-based questions from agents
- `waiting-for-human` label workflow
- Issue thread discussions for design decisions
- Workflow dispatch input overrides

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §4.3

title: [Phase 5.5] Document agent collaboration & populate .agent/ directory
body:
Complete the agent infrastructure:
- Create `.agent/skills/` directory with reusable skill documents
- Create `.agent/instructions/<category>/<package>/` structure
- Populate with initial instructions (e.g., `.agent/instructions/app-editors/zed/upgrade.md`)
- Document the agent collaboration process

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §2.1, §8.3

title: [Phase 6.1] Evaluate upstream release trigger options
body:
Research and document:
- `repository_dispatch` via external webhook approach
- Polling workflow approach
- Pros/cons of each
- Recommendation for implementation

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §5.1

title: [Phase 6.2] Implement chosen trigger mechanism for GitHub upstreams
body:
Implement the selected upstream release trigger mechanism:
- Set up webhooks or polling infrastructure
- Test with current packages
- Document configuration process

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §5.1

title: [Phase 6.3] Connect release triggers to upgrade workflow
body:
Wire the release trigger system to:
- Parse upstream release events
- Extract version information using patterns from packages.json
- Dispatch the upgrade-ebuild workflow automatically

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §5.1

title: [Phase 7.1] Update README.md with CI/CD documentation
body:
Update `README.md` to include:
- Overview of the CI/CD system
- How to use local development scripts
- Container environment documentation
- Links to detailed workflow documentation

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §9

title: [Phase 7.2] Add CONTRIBUTING.md for human and agent contributors
body:
Create `CONTRIBUTING.md` that documents:
- How to contribute as a human
- How agents interact with the repository
- Workflow for new ebuilds
- Workflow for ebuild upgrades
- Review and merge process

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md

title: [Phase 7.3] Create optional Makefile or Taskfile.yml
body:
Add a `Makefile` or `Taskfile.yml` that provides:
- Convenient aliases for common tasks
- `make lint PKG=...`
- `make test PKG=... EBUILD=...`
- `make check-updates`

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §9.3

title: [Phase 7.4] Review and harden all workflows
body:
Security and robustness review:
- Audit workflow permissions
- Verify secrets handling
- Improve error handling across all workflows
- Add retry logic where appropriate
- Document required secrets and permissions

Reference: planning/workflow_master_plan.md §12, §13
